project_title: Book Trilogy - Security, Dialectic, Repair : a three-book governed arc on dangerous power, democratic formation, and political repair after harm (the full manuscript corpus across 72 chapters)
runtime_target: chapter dossier system with argument, source, title, voice, visual, and handoff gates
operational_end_state: Each of 72 chapters carries a public title, argument backbone, source burden, book-specific voice rule, visual identity, revision status, and handoff to adjacent chapters.
minimum_operational_release: A chapter or manuscript release slice for Book Trilogy Manuscript Governance System: outline, argument state, source/passages used, revision rules, public title logic, and next reader-facing output.
full_build_definition: A manuscript system with chapter or section structure, source-governed argument logic, revision state, passage targets, reader-facing output, and publication-readiness checks.
source_actions_before_claim: Carry 7 source-application role(s) into the first build release and convert the most load-bearing source into a close-read contract before any quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
public_claim_now: May claim a full-build candidate exists with proof and source contracts; do not claim deployment, outside validation, or production readiness until live execution and passage verification are recorded.
current_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
blocking_gates: live capture and outside-use evidence before deployment or validation claims
first_full_build: Build one complete representative dossier per book and then scale the same fields across all 72 chapters.
launch_or_review_path: Open the manuscript/project contract for `trilogy`, select one chapter or section, and produce a source-bound release slice.

instruments.purpose: Turn the `chapter dossier registry` module into a concrete input-operation-output step for Book Trilogy Manuscript Governance System.
instruments.input_contract: Project fixture, source duty, current claim boundary, and required output state.
instruments.operation: Execute or inspect the chapter dossier registry step against the project fixture and record the state change it is supposed to produce.
instruments.output_contract: A reviewer-visible state record, output artifact, or decision boundary tied to the project acceptance tests.
instruments.acceptance_condition: The chapter dossier registry instrument is present, named, connected to the fixture, and has an output or decision field that can be reviewed.

acceptance_tests: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence
source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation
privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy
state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output
chapter uniqueness test: title, visual, source, and argument are chapter-specific

fixture.fixture_name: Book Trilogy Manuscript Governance System first operational slice
fixture.input: Build one complete representative dossier per book and then scale the same fields across all 72 chapters.
fixture.operation: Run the room engine through each required instrument, then produce a reviewer-visible state record and claim boundary.
fixture.expected_output: A chapter or manuscript release slice for Book Trilogy Manuscript Governance System: outline, argument state, source/passages used, revision rules, public title logic, and next reader-facing output.

export_schema.instrument_results: one result object per required module
export_schema.acceptance_results: one result object per required test
export_schema.claim_boundary: public claim ceiling after this run
export_schema.full_underlying_system_complete: always false unless backed by actual external runtime evidence
